meaning: n.<法> (政府对国民经济)干预或统制(主义)
examples: On industrial policy he remains wedded to dirigisme.
Less easy to shake off are the scheming and dirigisme.
But such harangues only exemplify the instinctive dirigisme that is a big part of Russia's problem.
In short, Mr Lee seems to be sticking to Singapore's special blend of dirigisme and free markets.
In keeping with the dirigisme of French agriculture, a regulatory body decrees which grapes can be planted where and how they can be used.
